nodes.context: Jewish-American street gang in Manhattan's Lower East Side, formed by Bugsy Siegel and Meyer Lansky, predecessor to Murder, Inc.
Enforcement outfit for the National Crime Syndicate, headed by Lepke Buchalter and Albert Anastasia.
Mob led by Joe Adonis, worked with Bugs and Meyer Mob in the 1920s.
Organized crime syndicate formed after the Castellammarese War, helped by Lansky and Siegel.
Crime syndicate formed by Lansky and Luciano in the early 1930s.

edges.evidence_quote: The Bugs and Meyer mob acted as a predecessor to Murder, Inc.
edges.context: Bugs and Meyer Mob was a predecessor to Murder, Inc.

edges.evidence_quote: Lansky and Siegel, being longtime associates of Luciano, would frequently employ the gang to work with Joe Adonis's Broadway Mob throughout the 1920s.
edges.context: Bugs and Meyer Mob worked with Broadway Mob in the 1920s.

edges.evidence_quote: During the Castellammarese War, Lansky and Siegel helped Luciano eliminate the "Mustache Petes" and organize the modern American Mafia.
edges.context: Bugs and Meyer Mob helped organize the modern American Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: When Lansky and Luciano formed the National Crime Syndicate in the early 1930s, Lansky, along with Siegel, pushed for a special outfit to handle "enforcement," or murders for the entire syndicate.
edges.context: Bugs and Meyer Mob members helped form the National Crime Syndicate.
